ogTitle: Woorinen South vs Wooroonook: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Woorinen South and Wooroonook.
heading: Woorinen South vs Wooroonook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Woorinen South, Victoria and Wooroonook, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wooroonook has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Woorinen South (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Woorinen South or Wooroonook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Woorinen South has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Wooroonook scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Woorinen South scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Wooroonook scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Woorinen South or Wooroonook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wooroonook has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
